diagnostic_artifacts.next_action: Use the USGS 3DEP ImageServer/WCS or a broader TNM product query for corridor DEM acquisition.
Use an official NAIP/USDA image service or product index query that returns California orthophoto tiles for the corridor.

production_blockers: Full corridor DEM/lidar tiles are not downloaded or conditioned yet.
Aerial imagery and water/vegetation masks are not downloaded yet.

Hydrography now has a bbox-intersected NHD HU8 18020129 source extract, an ordered South Fork mainstem candidate, preview metric stationing, cross-section seed lines, a preview NAIP/DEM alignment diagnostic, and generated production-import draft centerline/bank/cross-section overlays, but it still needs direction confirmation, production CRS selection, bank/cross-section review, stronger NAIP/DEM alignment, and guide review.

Modern release/gauge context is improved with a 30-day CDEC CBR/A25 window, but broader seasonal windows, A25 X-flag interpretation, station-to-reach routing, legal/redistribution signoff, and guide review are still required before flow bands are final.
No field/social reference media has explicit game-use rights attached yet.

caveats: Gauge history is historical, not a current live release feed.
Current South Fork commercial/release bands still require modern gauge/release context and guide review.
pulled_artifacts.provider: USGS Water Services / NWIS

caveats: This is negative availability evidence, not a modern flow record.
USGS 11445500 returned no instantaneous discharge or gage-height time series for the P30D request on 2026-07-06.
South Fork low/median/high visual and gameplay bands still need current official discharge, stage, release, or guide-validated flow context.

caveats: CBR is the primary official modern-flow candidate, not a promoted gameplay source yet.

The first CDEC terms/flag/station relation review is attached, but broader reproducible CSV/JSON pulls, release context, station-to-reach relation, legal/redistribution signoff, and guide/outfitter validation are required before flow bands are changed.
A25 powerhouse discharge is release-operation context and should not be used alone as raftable river flow without routing and guide review.

caveats: CDEC -9999 values are normalized to null and excluded from statistics.
The window includes a July 5 pulse near the median-runnable planning band and lower overnight values, but it is not enough to retune flow presets.

caveats: This is engineering source review, not legal signoff.
The first CBR window has only blank data flags after trimming, but broader windows may include flags requiring pipeline-specific handling.
The -9999 no-data sentinel is observed and normalized for the current artifact; official universal no-data semantics still require confirmation before broad automation.
CBR/A25 still need hydrologic routing, release context, and guide/outfitter validation before South Fork visual or gameplay flow bands change.

summary.station_relation_status: CBR primary candidate; A25 secondary release-operation context; direct flow-band authority still blocked

caveats: This is a compact summary artifact, not a raw telemetry archive.
CBR event-service rows returned a local 2026-06-06 23:00 first sample for the requested 2026-06-07 start.
A25 daily generation discharge includes X-flagged -9999 rows; X is not defined by the attached CDEC FlagList and is excluded pending CDEC/station review.
Do not retune presets or photoreal water appearance until broader seasonal windows, release/routing interpretation, legal/redistribution signoff, and guide review pass.

caveats: This is product metadata only; the selected HU8 shapefile extract is recorded separately and still needs review before production centerline/bank use.

preferred_product_kinds: National Hydrography Dataset Best Resolution GeoPackage
National Hydrography Dataset Best Resolution FileGDB

caveats: The extract is bbox-intersected full source geometry, not clipped at bbox edges.
The selected source CRS is NAD83 geographic degrees; reproject into the section working CRS before metric authoring.
The extracted NHD flowlines are not an ordered raftable centerline and do not include reviewed banks, shelves, boulders, eddy lines, or rapid features.
Waterbody/area polygon topology, islands, side channels, and seasonal exposure still need imagery, DEM, hydrology, and guide review.
pulled_artifacts.provider: USGS National Hydrography Dataset via The National Map

caveats: This is a derived candidate from named NHD South Fork American River segments, not a final raftable centerline.
East-to-west orientation is inferred for Chili Bar to Coloma and still needs confirmation against official flow direction, DEM slope, station context, and guide review.
The line is still in source geographic coordinates and must be reprojected before stationing, cross-section, solver, or Unreal transform work.
No reviewed banks, shelves, side channels, rapid boundaries, access points, or guide annotations are attached yet.
pulled_artifacts.provider: RaftSim derived artifact from USGS National Hydrography Dataset HU8 18020129

caveats: Uses a local equirectangular preview transform, not the final production working CRS.
Station zero and downstream direction are inherited from the inferred east-to-west mainstem candidate and still require confirmation.
Left normals are cross-section seeds only; banks, shelves, islands, and side channels are not reviewed.
Do not use for solver or Unreal coordinate authority until production CRS, NAIP/DEM alignment, and guide review pass.
pulled_artifacts.provider: RaftSim derived artifact from the South Fork NHD mainstem candidate

caveats: These are fixed-width review lines, not accepted banks, wetted widths, or solver cross sections.
Each line is 80 m to each side of the preview mainstem station sample and inherits the preview CRS/direction caveats.
The seed lines must be moved, clipped, removed, or supplemented against NAIP, 3DEP terrain, guide notes, rapid features, side channels, and access constraints before production use.
pulled_artifacts.provider: RaftSim derived artifact from the South Fork NHD stationing candidate

caveats: This is a preview diagnostic against review-gated masks, not an alignment acceptance result.
The water mask includes a bounded preview channel prior, so high agreement would not prove production bank alignment.
Only about 40 percent of in-bounds station samples exceed the 0.35 preview water threshold, so manual review and improved masks are still required.
Four station samples and three cross-section centers fall outside the current pilot bbox because source features are preserved whole near the downstream edge.
pulled_artifacts.provider: RaftSim diagnostic sampling from NHD stationing, cross-section seeds, NAIP preview masks, vegetation mask, and DEM relief

caveats: This is a rasterized NHD mainstem prior, not visible-water segmentation.
It agrees with the NHD stationing by construction and must not replace reviewed NAIP/DEM/bank masks.
It does not encode actual wetted width, shelves, side channels, islands, eddies, holes, flow level, or guide-reviewed hazards.
A few downstream station samples are clipped by the current pilot bbox because source features were preserved whole.
pulled_artifacts.provider: RaftSim derived artifact from the South Fork NHD mainstem stationing candidate

caveats: These are production-import folder overlays for editor review, not accepted route authority.
Bank lines are fixed-offset seed endpoints from cross-section review lines, not observed wetted banks.
Cross-section lines remain review seeds and must not be used as solver cross sections until bank, shelf, side-channel, and guide review pass.
pulled_artifacts.provider: RaftSim derived artifact from the South Fork NHD mainstem stationing and cross-section seeds
